title: Hoya gongshanica
intro: Hoya gongshanica, also known as the Gongshan waxy vine, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hoya, native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hoya gongshanica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hoya gongshanica is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Hoya gongshanica is a species within the Asclepiadaceae family, which is now more commonly referred to as Apocynaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Hoya gongshanica is a type of wax flower, known for its unique and fragrant blooms. Within the Apocynaceae family, Hoya gongshanica is classified as a member of the genus Hoya, which comprises over 500 species of climbing plants.
